title: Source to Shore spatial playthrough polish
summary: Play the three existing Source to Shore habitats as a physical route and tighten only the smallest spatial or visual cues needed for a steadier end-to-end beta.

execution_order.goal: Play through the existing Source Shelter, Forest Release, and Coastal Catch spaces and identify only real spatial readability issues.
execution_order.why_now: The next spatial work should make the current beta route steadier before adding more places.

execution_order.goal: Apply the smallest geometry, carrier, or proof changes needed to make the three-beat route read cleanly.
execution_order.why_now: A beta chapter needs a reliable physical memory arc, not only station and route-state correctness.

execution_order.goal: Review spatial polish with browser proof, science-safe carrier placement, and no new traversal-system drift.
execution_order.why_now: Spatial polish can sprawl quickly, so critique should confirm it stayed tied to the three existing beats.

completion_gate: Source Shelter, Forest Release, and Coastal Catch are each spatially readable in a representative 256x160 browser proof or equivalent focused test pass.
Any geometry or carrier changes are tiny, local, and science-safe.
No new cave, giant-tree system, traversal framework, biome, route beat, or player-physics change lands.
Focused biome/runtime/build checks pass if spatial files change.
guardrails: Polish the existing three beats only.
No new Source to Shore beat.
No new traversal system, cave system, biome, or physics pass.
Keep carrier placement honest to the local habitat.
Browser proof is required if visual geometry or placement changes land.

real_issue.description: At the right edge of the Treeline high-source memory pocket, the player can stand on the source-memory ptarmigan carrier while the `TO TUNDRA REACH` corridor prompt is already in range.

main_455_scope: Wait until `ECO-20260428-critic-450` clears the Source to Shore station-container gate.
Prefer a tiny local `src/content/biomes/treeline.ts` placement nudge so the Source Shelter memory carriers can be inspected before the Tundra corridor prompt appears.
Keep Forest Release and Coastal Catch unchanged unless fresh proof regresses them.
Re-run focused Treeline/Forest/Coastal biome coverage, the filed Source to Shore runtime smoke, `npm run build`, and fresh `256x160` browser proof if any geometry or carrier placement changes.
out_of_scope: No fourth Source to Shore beat.
No station, route-state, save-schema, support, world-map, player-physics, corridor-framework, cave-system, traversal-framework, or new-biome work.
Do not broaden the fix beyond the Treeline high-source prompt competition unless new proof shows another real spatial failure.

summary: Moved the Treeline Source Shelter high-source `source-memory-stone-step` platform from x=566 to x=563.
Moved the paired `source-memory-stone-boulder` carrier from x=570 to x=566 and `source-memory-fell-ptarmigan` carrier from x=580 to x=574.
Left Forest Release and Coastal Catch geometry unchanged because fresh proof still showed no travel prompt competition.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/treeline-biome.test.ts src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/coastal-scrub-biome.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts
out_of_scope_preserved: No station, route-state, save-schema, support, world-map, player-physics, corridor-framework, cave-system, traversal-framework, new-biome, or fourth-beat change landed.

review_summary: Runtime diff is limited to Treeline Source Shelter source-memory placement and its coordinate expectations.
Fresh 256x160 proof covers Source Shelter before prompt, Source Shelter at prompt edge, Forest Release, and Coastal Catch with no browser errors.
No station, route-state, save-schema, support, world-map, player-physics, corridor-framework, cave-system, traversal-framework, new-biome, or fourth-beat drift was found.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/treeline-biome.test.ts src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "source memory|Source to Shore memory pockets|high-source memory"
critic_review.final_signoff_dependency: Spatial gate is clear for `ECO-20260428-main-456`, but that lane-1 item remains parked until its other critic dependencies are clean.

post_packet_path: Feed proof artifacts into the final full-arc signoff packet.
Any larger spatial ambition should become a later lane-3 packet after beta signoff.
